footer.description: This institution follows the footsteps of the teacher of humanity, the messenger of liberation and peace, the ideal of human service, the Holy Prophet Muhammad (SAW), and is striving to build an ideal welfare society in serving the oppressed.

hero.title: Trump's announcement: US-Iran ceasefire ends, issues harsh military warning, extreme tension prevails in Middle East, global oil market affected

hero.slide4Title: Heavy casualties in Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon, extreme panic across Beirut, rescue operations underway for those trapped under rubble
hero.slide5Title: Severe food crisis in several African countries due to drought, millions at risk of starvation, urgent action needed from global community

descriptions: An important event has been completed that promises benefits for the community. This initiative is part of our ongoing commitment to serving the ummah and ensuring sustainable development. We hope this will bring long-lasting positive impacts to the community and inspire others to participate in our future endeavors. This comprehensive approach ensures that every aspect of the program is meticulously planned and executed. Our dedicated team of volunteers and professionals has worked tirelessly to bring this vision to life. By focusing on sustainable practices and long-term goals, we aim to uplift the marginalized and provide them with the tools they need to succeed. The overwhelmingly positive feedback from the community serves as a testament to the effectiveness of our efforts. We invite everyone to join hands with us as we continue to expand our reach and make a profound difference in the lives of many.

about.educationDesc: Establishing madrasas with integrated curriculum of religious and general education; establishing schools, colleges and universities along with various general and technical institutions; also initiating non-formal education
about.serviceDesc: Self-reliance of the poor, relief and rehabilitation of flood victims, providing medical services, supplying drinking water, distributing clothes and garments
about.dawahDesc: Writing and publishing books, mosque and auditorium-based activities, organizing seminars and conferences, dawah through media and social media

joinUs.heading: Join Us
joinUs.description: You can join us through any of the following methods and play a role in serving the oppressed.

media.description: A reliable archive of major media features, artistic recognition and global digital progress.

contact.heroDescription: We'd love to hear from you. Reach out to us with any questions, feedback, or support requests.
contact.name: Your Name
contact.subject: Subject
contact.addressValue: House 45, Road 11, Block D, Banani, Dhaka 1213.

items.a: Visit our activities page to learn more about our various programs.

items.q: How can I participate in your activities?
items.a: You can participate by registering through our website or contacting our office directly.

items.q: Are your activities open to everyone?
items.a: Yes, our activities are open to all community members regardless of background.
Contact us via phone or email, or visit our office directly.
Our office is open from 9 AM to 5 PM, Sunday through Thursday.

items.q: How can I reach you by email?
items.a: You can email us at info@mazlumummah.org and we will respond within 24 hours.

items.q: Want to know about Maulana Abdul Karim
items.a: Maulana Abdul Karim is our chief advisor. Visit our website to learn more about him.

items.q: What is Maulana Abdul Karim's background?
items.a: Maulana Abdul Karim has extensive knowledge in Islamic studies and has served the community for decades.

items.q: Can I get individual counseling from Maulana?
items.a: Yes, you can schedule an appointment through our contact page.
Contact our expert scholars for Sharia solutions and advice.

items.q: How do I submit a Sharia question?
items.a: You can submit your questions through our website form or by visiting our office.
Yes, all consultations and advice are kept strictly confidential.
Visit our donation page or contact us directly for any donation-related information.

items.q: What payment methods do you accept?
items.a: We accept bank transfers, mobile banking, and online payment methods.
Yes, donations are tax-deductible. You will receive a receipt for your contribution.
Email us if you would like to cooperate with us.
Yes, we welcome partnerships with other organizations that share our vision and values.

items.q: What types of cooperation do you offer?
items.a: We offer collaboration in humanitarian projects, educational programs, and community development initiatives.
Visit our membership page for details about membership.
Membership is free. We only ask for your commitment to our cause.

items.q: What are the benefits of membership?
items.a: Members receive regular updates, invitation to events, and the opportunity to participate in our programs.
Contact us to express your interest in volunteering.

items.q: What skills do I need to volunteer?
items.a: We welcome all skills. Specific requirements depend on the volunteer role.

items.q: How much time do I need to commit?
items.a: You can volunteer as much or as little as your schedule allows. Every contribution helps.
Check the notice board for recruitment notices.

items.q: How do I apply for a job?
items.a: Send your CV and cover letter to careers@mazlumummah.org.
Yes, we offer internship programs for students and recent graduates.

contact.guidelinesDesc: To make the most of Mazlum Ummah's services, please review the guidelines and rules below. Feel free to call us anytime for clarification or customization.
contact.guidelineDonationDesc: All donations to Mazlum Ummah are managed transparently. You can donate through bank transfer, mobile banking, or directly through our website.
contact.guidelineVolunteerDesc: To register as a volunteer, apply through our contact page. We welcome all volunteers who want to serve humanity.

bottomFaq.a: You can donate through our website's donation page or directly via bank transfer/mobile banking.
Yes, all donations are tax-deductible. You will receive a receipt for your contribution.
You can express your interest in volunteering by contacting us through our contact page. We welcome all volunteers.

bottomFaq.q: What payment methods do you accept?
bottomFaq.a: We accept bank transfers, mobile banking (bKash, Nagad, Rocket), and online payment methods.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.statementDesc: Mazlum Ummah Foundation is committed to protecting the privacy and security of your personal information. This privacy policy applies only to the Mazlum Ummah Foundation website and governs the use and storage of information collected from here. It does not apply to any other online or offline platforms, products or services.
privacy.reviewAdvice: We advise you to carefully review this policy before using the website.
privacy.dataCollectionDesc1: As a non-profit organization, Mazlum Ummah Foundation collects certain personal information, such as: name, email, phone number, address, etc. This information is used only for receiving donations, event registration and billing processes.
privacy.dataCollectionDesc2: We do not collect any information about your computer or device hardware and software.
privacy.dataCollectionDesc3: If you access another site through links on the website, please review their own privacy policy. We are not responsible for the privacy policies of third-party websites.
privacy.dataUsageIntro: Your provided personal information may be used:
dataUsageItems: To receive donations and complete transactions
To inform about new services, products or activities
In conducting surveys to gather user feedback and needs
privacy.dataUsageNoSell: We never sell, rent or transfer your information to third parties.
privacy.dataUsageThirdParty: However, we may engage reliable third parties to operate certain services (e.g., mail distribution, payment processing, technical assistance). In this case, they will only receive the necessary information and are contractually bound to keep it confidential.
privacy.dataUsageSensitive: We collect and use sensitive information (e.g., religion, ethnicity, political ideology) only on the basis of your explicit consent.
privacy.dataUsageDisclosure: Your information may be disclosed in case of legal requirements or emergency situations (e.g., ensuring user safety):
dataUsageDisclosureItems: To protect the foundation's rights and property
privacy.dataControlDesc1: You have control over the collection, use and sharing of your personal information. You can contact us at info@mazlumummah.org to request changes or deletion of your information.
privacy.dataControlDesc2: If you wish to stop receiving our promotional emails, you can remove yourself from our mailing list through the 'unsubscribe' option in any email.
privacy.dataSecurityDesc1: Mazlum Ummah Foundation is committed to keeping your personal information secure. We use various types of security technologies and measures to protect your information from unauthorized access, use or disclosure.
privacy.dataSecurityDesc2: For example, we store your personal information on secure servers where access is limited and under strict security. When we transmit highly sensitive information online (e.g., credit card numbers), we use a secure payment gateway to keep your information safe.
privacy.policyChangesDesc1: We may review and update this privacy policy from time to time. Any significant changes will be published on the website.
privacy.policyChangesDesc2: We request you to regularly review this policy so that you always stay updated.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.termsOfUseDesc: By visiting the Mazlum Ummah Foundation website, you agree to our terms of use. We advise you to review these terms carefully.
terms.trademarkDesc: Mazlum Ummah Foundation and other marks mentioned on this site are their respective trademarks owned by Mazlum Ummah Foundation. Use of these trademarks in any product or service without the foundation's approval is prohibited, if it may cause confusion or damage the foundation's reputation.
terms.copyrightDesc: All materials displayed on this website—such as text, graphics, logos, icons, images, audio-video clips, digital downloads and software—are exclusively the property of Mazlum Ummah Foundation. These materials are protected under Bangladesh and international copyright laws. The foundation reserves the right to take necessary legal action for any unauthorized use, copying or distribution of these materials.
terms.accountSecurityDesc: Users may need to create an account to access certain services on the website. The user is solely responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of account information, such as username and password. The user is responsible for all activities performed through their account.
terms.licenseDesc: Mazlum Ummah Foundation grants you a limited, non-transferable permission to use this website. Without the foundation's prior permission, downloading (except page caching), modifying, reproducing or using any material from the website for commercial purposes is strictly prohibited. Using the foundation's name in meta tags or hidden text is also included in this prohibition. If any rule is violated, your usage permission will be immediately considered revoked.
terms.userContentDesc1: If you submit any content on the website (e.g., opinions, videos or images, etc.), you retain ownership. However, by submitting it, you grant the foundation and its authorized associates a royalty-free, permanent and irrevocable license to use that content.
terms.userContentDesc2: You bear full responsibility for ensuring the accuracy and legal validity of submitted information or content. The foundation reserves the full right to delete any content at any time for public interest or policy requirements.
terms.refundPolicyDesc: If any donor wishes to return a donated contribution, the following terms will apply:
donationTypeDescs: Application must be submitted within 7-10 days of the donation with proper proof. However, if the money has been spent on a specific project within that time, it will no longer be eligible for refund.
Refund requests can be made at least 7 days before the Qurbani ceremony. Refund requests will not be accepted if delayed beyond this period.
To receive a refund, you must apply stating the specific reason and transaction ID.
terms.applicableLawDesc: By using the website, you agree that these terms of use and any disputes arising from them will be resolved in accordance with the laws of Bangladesh.
terms.policyChangesDesc: Mazlum Ummah Foundation reserves the right to modify, amend or revise these terms and policies at any time. This change will not apply to violations committed under the previous terms.

about.title: About Mazlum Ummah
about.summary: Mazlum Ummah is an initiative dedicated to humanitarian support, awareness, and social welfare.

sections.heading: Who We Are
paragraphs: Mazlum Ummah works to stand beside oppressed, vulnerable, and crisis-affected people. Our activities focus on humanitarian aid, dawah, education, health, emergency relief, and social welfare.
We believe coordinated effort and responsible support can bring meaningful assistance to people in need.

sections.heading: Our Purpose
paragraphs: Our purpose is to respond to humanitarian crises, publish reliable updates, and connect people with beneficial work.
items: Deliver support during disasters and crises

sections.heading: Transparency
paragraphs: Mazlum Ummah is committed to transparency and accountability toward donors, volunteers, and beneficiaries.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.summary: Protecting your personal information and privacy is our priority.

sections.heading: Privacy Statement
paragraphs: Mazlum Ummah is committed to protecting the privacy and security of your personal information. This policy explains how information collected through our website is used, stored, and protected.
Please review this policy carefully before using the website.

sections.heading: Information We Collect
paragraphs: We may collect your name, email, phone number, address, donation details, or communication details. This information is used for donations, communication, event registration, and service improvement.
Some technical data, such as browser, device, or visit information, may be used for security and analytics.

sections.heading: How We Use Information
paragraphs: Your information may be used to:

sections.heading: Selling or Sharing Data
paragraphs: We do not sell your personal information. Limited information may be shared with trusted service providers when required to operate services, and they must keep it confidential.

sections.heading: Your Rights
paragraphs: You may request correction, update, or deletion of your personal information by contacting us.

cookies.title: Cookies Policy
cookies.summary: Cookies may be used for website functionality, security, and usage analysis.

sections.heading: What Cookies Are
paragraphs: Cookies are small data files stored in your browser. They help maintain website functionality, user preferences, and security.

sections.heading: How We Use Cookies
paragraphs: Mazlum Ummah may use cookies to:
items: Understand visitor usage and improve the site

sections.heading: Your Control
paragraphs: You can disable or delete cookies from your browser settings. Some website features may not work correctly if certain cookies are disabled.

doNotShare.title: Do Not Share or Sell My Info
doNotShare.summary: You may request that your personal information is not sold or unnecessarily shared.

sections.heading: Our Position
paragraphs: Mazlum Ummah does not sell your personal information. We use your information only for service delivery, donation processing, communication, and legal requirements.

sections.heading: Limit Sharing Request
paragraphs: You may request that your personal information is not shared unnecessarily. We may ask for verification information before processing the request.

sections.heading: How to Request
paragraphs: Contact us with your name, contact details, and request details. We will review the request under applicable laws and policies.

externalLinks.title: External Links Policy
externalLinks.summary: Please review the relevant site policy before using external links from our website.

sections.heading: Third-Party Links
paragraphs: The Mazlum Ummah website may contain links to other websites, platforms, or services. These links are provided for user convenience.

sections.heading: Responsibility Limit
paragraphs: Mazlum Ummah is not responsible for third-party website content, security, privacy policies, or practices. Their own policies apply when you visit external sites.

sections.heading: Caution
paragraphs: Before providing personal information on an external link, review that site's privacy policy and terms.

mazlumPolicy.title: Mazlum Ummah Policy
mazlumPolicy.summary: General policy for website use, content, donations, and community conduct.

sections.heading: Terms of Use
paragraphs: By using the Mazlum Ummah website, you agree to follow our policies and terms. Website content is published for humanitarian, educational, and informational purposes.

sections.heading: Content Use
paragraphs: Website text, images, videos, graphics, and other materials may not be commercially used, modified, or republished without permission.

sections.heading: Donations and Activities
paragraphs: Users are responsible for providing accurate information when donating, registering as volunteers, or contacting us. Mazlum Ummah reserves the right to update activity, donation, or content policies when needed.

sections.heading: Safe Use
paragraphs: Harmful, misleading, unlawful, or rights-violating activity on the website is prohibited.

copyright.title: Copyright Policy
copyright.summary: Mazlum Ummah content, logos, images, and digital materials are protected by copyright.

sections.heading: Ownership
paragraphs: Text, images, videos, graphics, logos, icons, designs, and other digital materials published on this website are owned by Mazlum Ummah or used with permission.

sections.heading: Permitted Use
paragraphs: Limited quotation may be used for personal, educational, or news purposes if Mazlum Ummah is credited and the content is not distorted.

sections.heading: Prohibited Use
paragraphs: The following uses are prohibited without permission:
items: Publishing edited images, videos, or text in a misleading way
Using logos or brand materials without permission
Copying or republishing website content without authorization

sections.heading: Report Infringement
paragraphs: If you believe any content infringes your copyright, contact us with evidence. We will review the claim and take necessary action.
